target.title: fix(memory-core): exclude archive transcripts from dreaming corpus and propagate cron parentage to subagents
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix two defects in Dreaming session-corpus ingestion that cause cron-heavy deployment corpora to be dominated by automation noise (67%+ archive transcripts):
  1. **Archive re-ingestion**: `listSessionFilesForAgent` used `isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName` which includes `.reset.*` / `.deleted.*` archive artifacts. Archive exclusion is now at the Dreaming-only boundary — the shared helper remains usage-counted so `memory_search`/`QMD` can still index archives.
  2. **Cron parentage not propagated to subagents**: `isCronRunSessionKey` only matched the session's own key. Subagent sessions spawned by cron runs had `agent:main:subagent:*` keys and were never classified as cron-descended.
- The cron-descended classification now relies purely on the `spawnedBy` chain walk at runtime — no new durable session-store field is introduced.
- Fixes openclaw/openclaw#90313

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts` | Add archive transcript filter at Dreaming-only boundary (`isPrimarySessionTranscriptFileName` check); add secondary spawnedBy chain walk defense |
| `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.ts` | Keep `listSessionFilesForAgent` usage-counted for `memory_search`/`QMD` callers; add `spawnedBy` chain walk for cron-descended classification; remove `parentTrigger` handling |
| `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts` | Update tests — add chain-walk cron classification tests; remove `parentTrigger` test |
| `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/engine-qmd.ts` | Re-export session classification helpers for Dreaming boundary |
| `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/openclaw-runtime.ts` | Re-export `isPrimarySessionTranscriptFileName` for Dreaming boundary |
| `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/openclaw-runtime-session.ts` | Re-export session transcript helpers |
| `src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts` | Remove `parentTrigger` propagation (cron parentage now detected via spawnedBy chain walk only) |
| `src/config/sessions/types.ts` | Remove `parentTrigger` field from `SessionEntry` |
| `src/plugins/session-entry-slot-keys.ts` | Remove `parentTrigger` from reserved slot keys |

## Verification

- `pnpm test packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts` — 19/19 passed
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts` — 25/25 passed
- `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` — 47/47 passed
- Import cycles: clean
- Autoreview: clean — parentTrigger removal confirmed no longer needed; spawnedBy chain walk is the single canonical path
- CI: pre-existing TS2451 error on `main` (`src/commands/configure.shared.ts` duplicate `password` declaration) blocks full CI pipeline; all changed-code tests pass

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Exclude archive transcripts (`.reset.*` and `.deleted.*`) from the Dreaming ingestion corpus so cron-heavy workspaces do not have 67%+ archive contamination in Dreaming output. Also propagate cron parentage to subagent sessions so cron-descended subagents are correctly classified as cron runs — using only the existing `spawnedBy` chain, without adding new session-store fields.

**Real environment tested:** Local Linux x86_64 checkout of this PR branch (`fix/issue-90313-dreaming-corpus-archive`) on OpenClaw `2026.6.2` (build `05fea9a`). OpenClaw built from repo via `pnpm build`. Node 24.15.0 runtime. Proof script exercises actual built dist chunks (`paths-NEwU8m3X.js` and `engine-qmd-CtZpozMv.js`) against realistic session data.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. `pnpm build` — builds OpenClaw from PR branch source
2. `node /tmp/dreaming-proof.mjs` — runs the classification proof script that:
   a. Creates a temp state dir with: active transcripts, `.deleted.*` and `.reset.*` archive artifacts, cron-run session keys, subagent sessions with `spawnedBy` chains (including deep nesting), a normal session without cron ancestry, and a dreaming-narrative session
   b. Calls `isPrimarySessionTranscriptFileName()` and `isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName()` from the built dist
   c. Calls `loadSessionTranscriptClassificationForAgent()` from the built dist to classify all sessions
   d. Verifies archive files are excluded from the dreaming corpus
   e. Verifies cron-descended subagents (direct and deeply nested) are classified as cron-run
   f. Verifies normal subagents without cron ancestry are NOT classified as cron-run

**Evidence after fix:**
```
$ node /tmp/dreaming-proof.mjs

OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (05fea9a) — built from PR branch fix/issue-90313-dreaming-corpus-archive

=== 1. Archive exclusion via isPrimarySessionTranscriptFileName ===
  primary-session.jsonl
    → "active session transcript": primary=true, usage-counted=true
  user-session.jsonl.deleted.2026-06-15T10-00-00.000Z
    → "deleted archive artifact": primary=false, usage-counted=true
  user-session.jsonl.reset.2026-06-15T10-00-00.000Z
    → "reset archive artifact": primary=false, usage-counted=true
  user-session.jsonl.bak.2026-06-15T10-00-00.000Z
    → "compaction backup artifact": primary=false, usage-counted=false

=== 2. Session classification via spawnedBy chain walk ===
  cronRunTranscriptPaths (3):
    primary-session.jsonl
    cron-subagent.jsonl
    deep-subagent.jsonl
  dreamingNarrativeTranscriptPaths (1):
    dreaming-narrative.jsonl
  Normal subagent (no cron parentage) correctly excluded: true

=== 3. Archive files not in dreaming corpus ===
  EXCLUDED: user-session.jsonl.deleted.2026-06-15T10-00-00.000Z
  EXCLUDED: user-session.jsonl.reset.2026-06-15T10-00-00.000Z

=== VERDICT ===
  Cron-descended classified: 3/3 (direct cron + subagent + deep subagent)
  Dreaming narrative: 1/1
  Archive files excluded: 2/2
  Overall: PASS
```

**Observed result after fix:** All three fix behaviors verified against the built OpenClaw binary:
- `.deleted.*` and `.reset.*` archive transcripts return `primary=false` from `isPrimarySessionTranscriptFileName`, making them invisible to the Dreaming corpus collector while remaining `usage-counted=true` for `memory_search`/`QMD` archive indexing
- Direct cron sessions, subagent sessions with `spawnedBy → cron`, and deeply nested subagent sessions (subagent → subagent → cron) are all correctly classified as cron-run via the `spawnedBy` chain walk (3/3)
- Normal subagent sessions without cron ancestry are correctly NOT classified as cron-run
- Dreaming-narrative sessions retain their separate classification path

**What was not tested:** This proof was not run against a live remote OpenClaw workspace with real cron jobs. The changed functions are pure and deterministic (file-name predicates and session-store lookups), so unit test coverage (91 tests across 3 test files) plus this built-dist proof fully exercises both fix paths. The pre-fix evidence from issue #90313 documents a real cron-heavy workspace showing 67–91% daily archive contamination at `openclaw@2026.6.1`.

## Related

- Fixes openclaw/openclaw#90313
- Related: #67272, #68449, #72611 (distinct root causes — see issue for detailed distinction)
target.author: xialonglee
